statement: Trust in AI systems is becoming an evidence problem: durable trust will depend less on stated intent and more on independently repeatable assurance signals across evaluation, monitoring, and operational control.
rationale: Multiple fresh sources point toward operational evidence, evaluation, and monitoring as core trust mechanisms.

statement: Independent AI assurance will converge around continuously refreshed evidence bundles rather than one-time model reports.
Agentic AI trust failures will be easier to detect through workflow telemetry than through benchmark scores alone.
